mond Bowman of this city was re-
Gently announced, will be in Spo-
kane next week. She will be the

Z 4 &


MRS. SHRADER RETURNS.
Mrs. Pearl Hutton Shrader has
returned from a short trip to Lake
Pend Oreille. She visited the lake
last week with party of friends,
who made the trip in automobiles.


4 q 2


Is OCCUPYING SUMNER HOME
Mrs. Ethel Gordon has closed her
home in Brownies addition and Is
spending the summer months at her
cottage on the shores Of Liberty
lake. The quiet days are being
spent in company with number of
relatives from the east. who are her
guests for a brief length of time.


& e &


SPENT HONEYMOON IN CANADA
Mr.. and Mrs. T. P. Meyers have
returned to the states after an ex.
tended tour through eastern Canada
in an automobile. They are at pres
ent in Minnesota. Mrs. Meyers was
formerly Miss Lillian Brooks of this
city, whose marriage was an event
of the early summer.


& & &


WILL SPEAK FOR SUFFRAGE
Rev. Bruce Brown of the Dean
Avenue Christian church will speak
at meeting to be held tomorrow
evening at his church under the au-
spices of the Political Equality
league.


Rev. Brown, formerly of Colorado,
is an ardent believer in the cause
and will deliver splendid lecture
in their favor.


Mrs. Minnie ]. Reynolds of New
York will also speak.
